name: SUID Misconfiguration Privilege Escalation
description: Start as a low-privileged user on a Linux system. Enumerate SUID binaries, identify misconfigurations using GTFOBins techniques, and escalate to root to capture the flag in /root/flag.txt.

flags.hint: Run: find / -perm -u=s -type f 2>/dev/null — then check GTFOBins for each result you don't recognize as necessary.

learning_objectives: Understand what the SUID permission bit does and why it exists
Enumerate all SUID binaries using the find command
Distinguish between legitimate SUID binaries and misconfigurations
Use GTFOBins to find exploitation techniques for specific binaries
Escalate from www-data to root using a SUID binary
Understand how to correctly remediate SUID misconfigurations
Explain the principle of least privilege in the context of file permissions
